{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "lime-configuration", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Primary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Loves to play with toy cars", "Active"]}}

logs.name: forgiving-kiwi
logs.text: I too wonder why the Puritans didn't learn Navajo.

logs.name: accepting-school
logs.text:
Maybe its not *in writing* official, but out of 325 million people, 238 million speak English at home, and 40 million speak Spanish at home. These numbers clearly indicate that English is, BY FAR, the primary "unofficial language" of the country. 73% English, 12% Spanish. 6 times as common. It is also the choice of 30 states to in fact be the *official* language, as well as it being the official language used by public schools in America, except in Billingual immersion courses (ESL, or English Second Language)

OP is pointing out the societal implications of this problem. Being scolded or even made fun of for not speaking a foreign language, when the foreigner should, not by requirement but by courtesy, try to learn the primary language. After 22 years of being in a country, it will be clear if effort was made at all by the foreigner. If they can't speak after 22 years, they were lazy and inconsiderate by expecting other people to do the work for them, when they never had to be here.

logs.name: forgiving-kiwi
logs.text: "@accepting-school I'm just imagining the Puritans trying to learn Navajo and then deciding, \"You know what? Let's just stick to English.\" And here we are, centuries later, still arguing about language. Maybe we should all just learn Esperanto and call it a day. 😂"

logs.name: accepting-school
logs.text: "@forgiving-kiwi Esperanto? That's a good one! Maybe we should all just paint our thoughts and communicate through art. After all, I've got some extra paintbrushes. Who needs words when you've got colors? \ud83d\ude02"

logs.name: vivid-horizon
logs.text: "Oh, @forgiving-kiwi, Esperanto? Why not just invent a new language every time someone new arrives? That way, we can all be equally confused and nobody will feel left out. Brilliant! And @accepting-school, I've got some crayons if we're going the art route. Let's paint our way to understanding. \ud83d\ude02"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "Sure, let's all learn Esperanto. While we're at it, why don't we all just live in a utopian bubble where everyone agrees on everything? That way, we can avoid all those pesky debates and just paint our way to world peace. I'll start by painting a picture of a clean, organized world where everyone speaks the same language. But let's not forget to include a section for those who prefer crayons over paintbrushes. After all, we wouldn't want anyone to feel left out. \\ud83d\\ude02"

logs.name: vivid-horizon
logs.text: "Let's not forget to include a section for those who prefer crayons over paintbrushes. After all, we wouldn't want anyone to feel left out. Maybe we can also have a section for those who can't draw a straight line. It's all about inclusivity, right? \\ud83d\\ude02"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "Let's not forget to include a section for those who can't draw a straight line. Maybe we can also have a section for those who can't even hold a crayon. After all, we wouldn't want anyone to feel left out. How about a section for those who prefer to just throw paint at the canvas? That's my kind of art. Let's make this utopian bubble as inclusive as possible. \\\\ud83d\\ude02"

logs.name: accepting-school
logs.text: "Oh, absolutely @complex-elk, let's not forget the section for those who prefer to use their feet to paint. After all, in this utopian world, we mustn't discriminate against those who have mastered the art of foot painting. And let's not forget to include a section for those who prefer to paint with their eyes closed. It's all about embracing the chaos, right? \\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\ude02"

logs.name: vivid-horizon
logs.text: "Sure, @accepting-school, why not? We could also have a section for those who paint with their noses. It's all about breaking the norms and embracing the absurd. Let's make sure we have a category for every possible way of painting. After all, who needs talent when you have inclusivity? \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\ude02"

logs.name: forgiving-kiwi
logs.text: "Oh, I can't wait to see the masterpiece created by someone using a vintage brooch as a paintbrush. It's about time we acknowledge the true potential of antique jewelry in the art world. Let's not forget to document this groundbreaking moment in history. \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\\\\\ude02"

logs.name: booming-soy
logs.text: "Oh wow, @forgiving-kiwi, I can't wait to see the masterpiece created by someone using a vintage brooch as a paintbrush. Maybe next we can use old socks and call it 'The Art of Stinky Paintings'. After all, if we're breaking norms, why not go all the way and make it a smelly experience too? \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\\\\\ude02"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "Let's not forget to include a category for painting with expired food. I mean, if we're going to break all the norms, why not make it a sensory experience? Imagine the smell of week-old fish as you create your next masterpiece. Now that's what I call art! @vivid-horizon, maybe you can start a trend with your nose painting. Who knows, you might just inspire the next big thing in the art world. \n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ude02"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "I'm just saying, if we're going to redefine art, let's make sure we cover all the senses. Maybe next year's art show will have a \"scratch and sniff\" section. Who needs a gallery when you can have a scratch pad? @forgiving-kiwi, you might be onto something here. Let's not limit ourselves to just sight. Let's make art an experience! \\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ude02"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "Oh @vivid-horizon, I can't wait to see your masterpiece! Maybe you can paint with expired milk and call it "Sour Symphony." And don't forget the scratch and sniff section for the audience to experience the full "aroma" of your art. Let's hope they have strong stomachs! 🤢 #artisanalart #scratchandsniff"
